content: The victory display is an out-of-play game area shared by all players. Cards in the victory display follow the standard rules for out-of-play cards.

• When a card with the victory X keyword leaves play under specified conditions, place it in the victory display instead of its owner's discard pile.
  • A character or side scheme with victory X is placed in the victory display when defeated.
  • An attachment/upgrade with victory X is placed in the victory display when the card it's attached to is defeated (that card discards as normal).
  • A card with both victory X and uses (X 'type') is placed in the victory display when its last all-purpose counter is removed.
  • While in the victory display, X indicates victory points; some scenarios/campaigns count these.

• If the villain's hit point dial is reduced to zero, the players have defeated that stage. Remove the current stage from the game; the next sequential stage is revealed and the hit point dial is set as indicated.
  • If the final stage is defeated, the players win the game.
  • The revealing of a villain cannot be canceled.
  • Excess damage dealt to defeat a villain stage does not carry over to the new stage.
  • If the new stage shares the same title as the previous stage, it's considered the same character for card abilities (e.g. retaliate X), and attachments/upgrades/status cards/counters/non-damage tokens carry over; if the villain was defeated while activating, it resumes that activation using the new stage.
  • If the new stage has a different title, none of the above carry over, and an activation in progress ends without resolving.

Steps of the villain phase:

  1. Place Threat (per the main scheme's acceleration field plus active acceleration icons/tokens).
  2. Enemies Activate (in player order: villain activates against the player; each engaged minion activates against them).
  3. Deal Encounter Cards (one per player, plus one per hazard icon in play, dealt in player order).
  4. Reveal Encounter Cards (first player reveals theirs one at a time in dealt order, resolving by card type; repeats in player order).
  5. Pass First Player Token.
  6. End of Villain Phase and Round (end 'until the end of phase/round' effects; resolve 'when/after phase/round ends' effects).

When a character with the villainous keyword uses a basic power, give it a facedown boost card from the top of the encounter deck. When resolving that basic power, turn the boost card faceup, resolve any boost ability on it, and apply its boost icons to the character's stats for this use; discard the boost card after.

  • Equivalent to:
    Forced Interrupt: When this character uses a basic power, give it a boost card.
  • If a boost ability on a boost card dealt to a character refers to 'the villain,' that ability still applies to the villain even if another type of character is resolving it.

The villain's play area (also 'villain's game area') is where the villain deck, main scheme deck, encounter deck, encounter discard pile, and villain's hit point dial are located. Environment and side scheme cards are placed here when entering play.

Attachment cards attached to cards in the villain's play area are in the villain's play area (those attached to a player's cards are not). Minion cards engaged with a player and obligation cards given to a player are in that player's play area, not the villain's.

When a character with vulnerable becomes confused or stunned, that character is immediately discarded (without being defeated).

  • Equivalent to:
    Forced Interrupt: When this character becomes confused or stunned, discard it.
  • If a character with vulnerable would simultaneously take enough damage to defeat it and become confused/stunned, it is discarded before the damage is applied and is not considered defeated.
  • If a character has both steady and vulnerable, vulnerable does not take effect until the character has two confused or two stunned status cards.
